# newsletter [DokuWiki]

newsletter

DokuWiki Newsletter
===================

For a couple of years we ran a semi-regular [DokuWiki](https://www.dokuwiki.org/dokuwiki "dokuwiki")
 newsletter. However, due to the lack of voluntary writers the newsletters became more infrequent until we finally gave up on them all together.

If you like to stay up to date with what's happening in DokuWiki world, it's recommended to follow us via our [social media](https://www.dokuwiki.org/social "social")
 accounts

Longer posts on DokuWiki topics can also be found on [Andi's patreon page](https://www.patreon.com/dokuwiki "https://www.patreon.com/dokuwiki")
.

Edit

Past Issues
-----------

An incomplete [DokuWiki Newsletter Archive of old newsletters](https://us1.campaign-archive.com/home/?u=72ec38d1614d3a63c1184f448&id=9c27f4f01d "https://us1.campaign-archive.com/home/?u=72ec38d1614d3a63c1184f448&id=9c27f4f01d")
 is still available.

Edit

# it:localization [DokuWiki]

it:localization

### Indice

*   [Localizzare DokuWiki (il motore)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/it:localization#localizzare_dokuwiki_il_motore)
    
*   [Tradurre DokuWiki.org (la documentazione)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/it:localization#tradurre_dokuwikiorg_la_documentazione)
    
    *   [Linee guida per le traduzioni](https://www.dokuwiki.org/it:localization#linee_guida_per_le_traduzioni)
        
    *   [Disabilitare la funzione di traduzione per alcune pagine](https://www.dokuwiki.org/it:localization#disabilitare_la_funzione_di_traduzione_per_alcune_pagine)
        
*   [Si veda anche](https://www.dokuwiki.org/it:localization#si_veda_anche)
    

Localizzare DokuWiki (il motore)
================================

L'interfaccia utente di [DokuWiki](https://www.dokuwiki.org/it:dokuwiki "it:dokuwiki")
 può essere configurata per usare qualsiasi lingua impostando l'appropriata opzione di [config](https://www.dokuwiki.org/config:lang "config:lang")
.

Una lista delle interfacce tradotte disponibili si trova su [DokuWiki Localization Hub](http://translate.dokuwiki.org/ "http://translate.dokuwiki.org")
.

Per tradurre DokuWiki in un'altra lingua, si visiti [http://translate.dokuwiki.org/](http://translate.dokuwiki.org/ "http://translate.dokuwiki.org/")
, si scelga o si aggiunga una lingua e si usi l'editore web delle traduzioni per creare o aggiornare un pacchetto lingua.

Se si effettua una traduzione basta semplicemente spedirla a [andi \[at\] splitbrain \[dot\] org](mailto:andi%20%5Bat%5D%20splitbrain%20%5Bdot%5D%20org "andi [at] splitbrain [dot] org")
 e sarà inclusa nella successiva versione.

I traduttori dovrebbero unirsi alla [I18N Mailing List](https://www.dokuwiki.org/it:mailinglist#i18n_mailing_list "it:mailinglist")
 per essere informati quando nuove stringhe vengono aggiunte e necessitano di essere tradotte.

Modifica

Tradurre DokuWiki.org (la documentazione)
=========================================

Tradurre la documentazione di DokuWiki su dokuwiki.org è incoraggiato. Le pagine tradotte devono avere lo stesso nome delle loro corrispondenti in Inglese ed essere archiviate in una distinta categoria basata sul proprio codice ISO. Per rendere questo più facile [translation plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:translation "plugin:translation")
 aggiunge collegamenti alle esistenti e mancati traduzioni nell'angolo superiore destro di ogni pagina.

Solo le lingue configurate manualmente dall'amministratore sono mostrate nel selettore. Se si vuole aggiungere traduzioni per una lingua attualmente non mostrata, basta creare manualmente le pagine nell'appropriata categoria della lingua e inviare un messaggio di posta elettronica alla [I18N mailing list](https://www.dokuwiki.org/it:mailinglist#i18n_mailing_list "it:mailinglist")
 chiedendo che la lingua sia aggiunta.

Modifica

Linee guida per le traduzioni
-----------------------------

*   Ci si senta liberi di creare qualsiasi traduzione mancante
    
*   Solo le pagine nella categorie superiore dovrebbero essere tradotte
    
*   Le pagine tradotte devono avere lo stesso nome (inglese) della pagina originale
    
*   Le pagine tradotte devono essere memorizzate in una sotto categoria della categoria radice chiamata con il nome del codice ISO della lingua.
    
*   Si prega di non aggiungere ceppi dicendo che la pagina ancora non esiste
    
*   Si prega di non ingombrare le traduzioni con righe di credito – Se si vuole essere riconosciuti come traduttori si esegua prima l'accesso.
    
*   Un buon punto di partenza per le traduzioni è il [manuale](https://www.dokuwiki.org/it:manual "it:manual")
    

Modifica

Disabilitare la funzione di traduzione per alcune pagine
--------------------------------------------------------

Quando la traduzione non è sensibile la macro ~~NOTRANS~~ dovrebbe essere inserita nella pagina.

Modifica

Si veda anche
=============

*   [DokuWiki Localization Hub](http://translate.dokuwiki.org/ "http://translate.dokuwiki.org")
    
*   [Romanizzazione](https://www.dokuwiki.org/it:romanization "it:romanization")
    
*   [Translation Plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:translation "plugin:translation")
    

Modifica

Add New Page Plugin
===================

Compatible with DokuWiki

*   2025-05-14 "Librarian" **probably**
*   2024-02-06 "Kaos" **yes**
*   2023-04-04 "Jack Jackrum" **yes**
*   2022-07-31 "Igor" **yes**

*   [Download](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-addnewpage/zipball/master)
    
*   [Report bugs](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-addnewpage/issues)
    
*   [Donate](https://github.com/sponsors/dregad)
    

[![plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/plugins/pluginrepo/images/dwplugin.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins)
 Put ‘add new page’ forms within pages, with optional namespace selection

[![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_media/plugin:addnewpage_ns_nothidden.png?w=220&tok=1bef96)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_media/plugin:addnewpage_ns_nothidden.png "Screenshot of addnewpage")

Last updated on

2025-04-22

Provides

[Syntax](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintype=1#extension__table "List all Syntax plugins")

Repository

[Source](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-addnewpage)

Conflicts with

[infomail](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:infomail "plugin:infomail")

Similar to [catlist](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:catlist "plugin:catlist")
, [copypage](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:copypage "plugin:copypage")
, [jsongendoc](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:jsongendoc "plugin:jsongendoc")
, [new\_page\_dialog](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:new_page_dialog "plugin:new_page_dialog")
, [npd](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:npd "plugin:npd")
, [pagehere](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:pagehere "plugin:pagehere")

Tagged with [button](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintag=button#extension__table "List all plugins with this tag")
, [create](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintag=create#extension__table "List all plugins with this tag")
, [form](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintag=form#extension__table "List all plugins with this tag")

Needed for [autoincludeindex](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:autoincludeindex "plugin:autoincludeindex")

**By [Damien Regad, Benjamin Santalucia, Sam Wilson and other contributors](mailto:dregad%20%5Bat%5D%20mantisbt%20%5Bdot%5D%20org "dregad [at] mantisbt [dot] org")
**

*   [newpagetemplate](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:newpagetemplate "plugin:newpagetemplate")
    
*   [cspheader](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:cspheader "plugin:cspheader")
    
*   [authwordpress](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress "plugin:authwordpress")
    
*   [deletepagebutton](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:deletepagebutton "plugin:deletepagebutton")
    

Edit

Installation
------------

Search and install the plugin using the [Extension Manager](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:extension "plugin:extension")
. Refer to [Plugins](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins "plugins")
 on how to install plugins manually.

Edit

Description
-----------

This plugin allows you to add new-page forms to wiki pages, so that users can simply enter the title of a new page and go straight to the editing form (rather than first creating a link to the page, or typing the URL directly).

The target namespace for the new page can either be selected from a drop-down list in the new-page form, or specified in the form [syntax](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpage#syntax "plugin:addnewpage ↵")
. Only namespaces to which the user is permitted to write are shown.

The plugin can pair with the [newpagetemplate Plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:newpagetemplate "plugin:newpagetemplate")
 to start with a predefined template instead of an empty page.

The form submit button is disabled if there is no text in the title field.

Edit

Usage
-----

Put `{{NEWPAGE}}` anywhere in a wiki page to get a new-page-title text entry box, and a namespace selection drop-down menu. More details in the [Syntax](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpage#syntax "plugin:addnewpage ↵")
 section below.

Edit

### Screenshots

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| [![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_media/plugin:addnewpage_nooptions.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_detail/plugin:addnewpage_nooptions.png?id=plugin%3Aaddnewpage "plugin:addnewpage_nooptions.png") | The default form has a drop-down with all namespaces of the wiki and a text field for the pagename. |
| [![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_media/plugin:addnewpage_ns_hidden.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_detail/plugin:addnewpage_ns_hidden.png?id=plugin%3Aaddnewpage "plugin:addnewpage_ns_hidden.png") | `{{NEWPAGE>test}}` with configuration option “Hide namespace selection” enabled. |
| [![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_media/plugin:addnewpage_ns_nothidden.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_detail/plugin:addnewpage_ns_nothidden.png?id=plugin%3Aaddnewpage "plugin:addnewpage_ns_nothidden.png") | `{{NEWPAGE>test}}` with configuration option “Hide namespace selection” disabled. |
| [![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/exe/fetch.php?tok=2d306d&media=http%3A%2F%2Fi.imgur.com%2FgQ6yl0Y.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/exe/fetch.php?tok=2d306d&media=http%3A%2F%2Fi.imgur.com%2FgQ6yl0Y.png "http://i.imgur.com/gQ6yl0Y.png") | Parent namespaces are greyed-out where the user has no access to add pages. |

Edit

Edit

Syntax
------

Edit

### Basic

The basic syntax is:

{{NEWPAGE}} 

Or, to specify a namespace:

{{NEWPAGE>namespace}}

Where `namespace` is the target namespace for the new page. When this is specified, the namespace selection box can either be hidden (which it is by default) or set to show only sub-namespaces of the given one. This is configured in the main wiki configuration manager. If `@PAGE@` (or `@NS@`) is given, the current page ID (or its namespace) will be used (e.g. for use in [namespace templates](https://www.dokuwiki.org/namespace_templates "namespace_templates")
; see below).

Edit

### Creating a namespace

It is possible to use the `@INPUT@` placeholder to reference the user's input in the namespace specification. For example,

{{NEWPAGE>foo:@INPUT@:start}}

will create a new namespace within the _foo_ namespace.

Edit

### Date/time-based naming

The plugin supports usage of [strftime placeholders](https://www.php.net/manual/en/function.strftime.php "https://www.php.net/manual/en/function.strftime.php")
 in the namespace config. This allows adding date elements to the namespace specification

{{NEWPAGE>project:notes:@INPUT@\_%Y-%m-%d}}
{{NEWPAGE>reports:%Y:week\_%W}}

When used together with the [autopage](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpage#configuration_and_settings "plugin:addnewpage ↵")
 option to hide the input field, a daily page can be created with a single button click:

{{NEWPAGE>journal:%Y-%m-%d?autopage}}

Edit

### Configuration overrides

It is possible to override the plugin's [configuration](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpage#configuration_and_settings "plugin:addnewpage ↵")
 for a single usage of the syntax, by specifying a `?` followed by the config's name with an optional `no` prefix to negate it. Values for non-boolean configs can be provided with `=`, separated by `;`. Multiple overrides can be given, separated by `,` or `&`.

General syntax:

?\[no\]<option\_1>\[=value\_1\[;value\_2\]...\]\[,\[no\]<option\_2>\]...

Examples:

*   overwrite the default hide setting
    
    {{NEWPAGE>namespace?nohide}}
    
*   custom namespace exclusion and hide root
    
    {{NEWPAGE>namespace?exclude=project;reports&noshowroot}}
    

Edit

### Custom button labels

The submit button's label can be changed from the default _Add Page_, to a text of your choice using the 'label' option

{{NEWPAGE?label=Custom Label}}

[![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/exe/fetch.php?tok=4e988f&media=https%3A%2F%2Fi.postimg.cc%2F3wXDLDZc%2Fimage.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/exe/fetch.php?tok=4e988f&media=https%3A%2F%2Fi.postimg.cc%2F3wXDLDZc%2Fimage.png "https://i.postimg.cc/3wXDLDZc/image.png")

Custom labels can of course be combined with other parameters, allowing for example to provide a one-click button to create a journal page.

{{NEWPAGE>journal:%Y-%m-%d?autopage&label=New Journal Page}}

[![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/exe/fetch.php?tok=ca917b&media=https%3A%2F%2Fi.postimg.cc%2FtJdsk1Gz%2Fimage.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/exe/fetch.php?tok=ca917b&media=https%3A%2F%2Fi.postimg.cc%2FtJdsk1Gz%2Fimage.png "https://i.postimg.cc/tJdsk1Gz/image.png")

Edit

### New-page templates

This plugin is also compatible with the [newpagetemplate plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:newpagetemplate "plugin:newpagetemplate")
 (note that this is a separate feature to the [namespace templates](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpage#namespace_templates "plugin:addnewpage ↵")
 described below). To specify a template to be used with the new page, use one of the following syntaxes:

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| `{{NEWPAGE#newtpl}}` | Use the `newtpl` template |
| `{{NEWPAGE#newtpl1,newtpl2}}` | Present a drop-down list of `newtpl1` and  <br>`newtpl2`, allowing the user to choose one |
| `{{NEWPAGE#newtpl1\|Title One,newtpl2\|Title Two}}` | The same as above, but with nicer titles |

Edit

In all of these, the basic `>namespace` syntax can be included before the hash `#` character.

Edit

### Multiple occurences

The `{{NEWPAGE}}` syntax may be added multiple times on the same page.

Edit

### Site templates

To include an add-new-page form in a [Site Template](https://www.dokuwiki.org/template "template")
, use the following line:

<?php echo p\_render('xhtml',p\_get\_instructions('{{NEWPAGE}}'),$info) ?>	

Edit

### Namespace Templates

Add-new-page forms can also be added to [Namespace Templates](https://www.dokuwiki.org/namespace_templates "namespace_templates")
. The replacement patterns `@ID@` and `@NS@` can be used in the plugin's `namespace` parameter.

Edit

Configuration and Settings
--------------------------

You can configure the plugin in the Config Manager of DokuWiki. There are four settings:

1.  **Excluded namespaces** (`exclude`) — A semicolon-separated list of namespaces that should be excluded from the namespace selection list. Default: `wiki;playground`.
    
2.  **Show root namespace** (`showroot`) — Whether to show the wiki's root namespace in the namespace selection list. Default: true.
    
3.  **Hide namespace selection** (`hide`) — Hide the namespace selection list. Only applies if a namespace is given (see [above](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpage#syntax "plugin:addnewpage ↵")
    ). Default: true.
    
4.  **Hide “access denied” message** (`hideACL`) — Do not show the “You are not allowed to add pages” message when the user doesn't have permission to create pages. Default: false (i.e. do show the message).
    
5.  **Hide the input box** (`autopage`) — The preconfigured namespace is treated as a full page ID. Use with [date placeholders](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpage#date_time-based_naming "plugin:addnewpage ↵")
    ). Default: false.
    

Edit

FAQ
---

#### Why is a privileged user not allowed to add pages?

Logged in as administrator, why do I get a _You are not allowed to add pages_ error message ?

> This is a [known problem](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-addnewpage/issues/38 "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-addnewpage/issues/38")
> , caused by caching. As a workaround, add `~~NOCACHE~~` to the page that contains the form.

#### Addnewpage form on the admin page?

Is there a possibility to add the plugin as a part of the admin page, like the plugins pagemove or upgrade?

> Not currently, but this can be done by modifying your template and including the addnewpage form only when `$ACT=='admin`'

#### More than one form per page?

Is it possible to have more than one new-page form on a page?

> Yes. Nothing special needs to be done to permit this, just add the markup wherever you want the forms to appear.

#### Can the textbox be pre-populated with a value? Specifically, the ID variable? (Trying to have a user search for a page, then use this plugin to create the page if they don't find what they're looking for...)

#### How can a add\_page form add in the site template automaticaly? Where to place the php statements?

Edit

Development
-----------

Edit

.

Items of general interest should be added to the [FAQ above](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpage#faq "plugin:addnewpage ↵")
.

Edit

### Legacy releases

#### Before development moved to Github

*   2008-01-10: New zip file with some correction (the submit bug in IE, the namespace “.”) and all new language are added
    
*   2007-05-19: Portuguese language definitions added for completely hide NEWPAGE by GibaPhp
    
*   2007-01-21: Can configure if you want to add some message about rights or completely hide NEWPAGE
    
*   2007-01-21: Now support ACL (thanks to 66\[dot\]7\[dot\]118\[dot\]82)
    
*   2007-01-13: Add new functionality with the syntax {{NEWPAGE\[>namespace\]}} (thanks to Martin)
    
*   2007-01-12: Remove 4 invisible hex chars in script.js, adding Italian and Russian (incomplete)
    
*   2007-01-11: Fix encoding problem (with old noaccent function)
    
*   2006-12-20: New Release, old version \[used to be\] at `plugin:addnewpage_old`
    

#### When this plugin was 'addnewpage\_old'

*   2006-11-14: Fixed: bug with new dokuwiki version. Added italian language file.
    
*   2006-07-05: Fixed: Zip file is now ok. Added: Russian language file.
    
*   2006-06-20 : Fixed: Problem with the empty select box, Added: Multilanguage Support; Root Namespace (see Kibi's comment); The selected line is now the same as the current namespace (see Kibi's comment);
    

Edit

History / Credits
-----------------

The plugin was [originally written](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpage_old?rev=1366805333 "plugin:addnewpage_old")
 by Benjamin Santalucia (aka iDo) in 2006. It was forked after the 2009-02-14 release of Dokuwiki, and moved to GitHub by [Robert McLeod](https://github.com/hamstar "https://github.com/hamstar")
 in 2012. Refer to the [Legacy Releases](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpage#legacy_releases "plugin:addnewpage ↵")
 section for details on what happened in those early, pre-GitHub years.

In 2013, [Sam Wilson](https://github.com/samwilson "https://github.com/samwilson")
 picked up the plugin's maintenance and actively supported it until he stopped using DokuWiki at the end of 2016. During this time, he merged several improvements and fixes contributed by these fine people:

*   Michael Braun
    
*   Róbert Toth (FurloSK)
    
*   Gerrit Uitslag ([Klap-in](https://forum.dokuwiki.org/u/Klap-in "https://forum.dokuwiki.org/u/Klap-in")
    ) added support for [newpagetemplate](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:newpagetemplate "plugin:newpagetemplate")
     plugin in 2014
    
*   Albert Chern, disabling of restricted parent namespaces in 2015
    

In May 2023, Damien Regad decided to take over the plugin's maintenance.

Thanks to [all the people who contributed](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-addnewpage/graphs/contributors "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-addnewpage/graphs/contributors")
 to this plugin over the years.

Edit

# de:devel:syntax_plugins [DokuWiki]

de:devel:syntax\_plugins

Media Manager
=============

Namespaces
----------

plugin:addnewpagedeluxe

addnewpagedeluxe Plugin
=======================

Compatible with DokuWiki

*   2025-05-14 "Librarian" **unknown**
*   2024-02-06 "Kaos" **yes**
*   2023-04-04 "Jack Jackrum" **yes**
*   2022-07-31 "Igor" **yes**

*   [Download](https://github.com/C0DE8/dokuwiki-plugin-addnewpagedeluxe/zipball/master)
    
*   [Report bugs](https://github.com/C0DE8/dokuwiki-plugin-addnewpagedeluxe/issues)
    
*   [Donate](https://ko-fi.com/C0DE8)
    

[![plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/plugins/pluginrepo/images/dwplugin.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins)
 Put ‘add new page’ (deluxe) forms within pages, with optional namespace selection

Last updated on

2024-02-26

Provides

[Syntax](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintype=1#extension__table "List all Syntax plugins")

Repository

[Source](https://github.com/C0DE8/dokuwiki-plugin-addnewpagedeluxe/)

Similar to [new\_page\_dialog](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:new_page_dialog "plugin:new_page_dialog")
, [npd](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:npd "plugin:npd")

Tagged with [button](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintag=button#extension__table "List all plugins with this tag")
, [create](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintag=create#extension__table "List all plugins with this tag")
, [form](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintag=form#extension__table "List all plugins with this tag")

**By [C0DE8](mailto:dokuwiki%20%5Bdot%5D%20development%20%5Bat%5D%20c0de8%20%5Bdot%5D%20com "dokuwiki [dot] development [at] c0de8 [dot] com")
**

*   [breadcrumbdeluxe](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:breadcrumbdeluxe "plugin:breadcrumbdeluxe")
    

**NOTE:** This Plugin is derived/forked from the original [Add New Page Plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpage "plugin:addnewpage")
 and _pretends to_ extend the functionality _but actually does not, and (as of 2025-01-03) includes bugs that have been fixed upstream, e.g. [#112](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-addnewpage/issues/112 "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-addnewpage/issues/112")
_.

_Disclaimer: Text in italics added by maintainer of original [Add New Page Plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpage "plugin:addnewpage")
_.

Edit

Installation
------------

Search and install the plugin using the [Extension Manager](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:extension "plugin:extension")
. Refer to [Plugins](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins "plugins")
 on how to install plugins manually.

Edit

Description
-----------

This plugin allows you to add new-page forms to wiki pages, so that users can simply enter the title of a new page and go straight to the editing form (rather than first creating a link to the page, or typing the URL directly).

The target namespace for the new page can either be selected from a drop-down list in the new-page form, or specified in the form [syntax](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpagedeluxe#syntax "plugin:addnewpagedeluxe ↵")
. Only namespaces to which the user is permitted to write are shown.

The plugin can pair with the [newpagetemplate Plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:newpagetemplate "plugin:newpagetemplate")
 to start with a predefined template instead of an empty page.

The form submit button is disabled if there is no text in the title field.

Edit

Usage
-----

Put `{{NEWPAGE}}` anywhere in a wiki page to get a new-page-title text entry box, and a namespace selection drop-down menu. More details in the [Syntax](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpagedeluxe#syntax "plugin:addnewpagedeluxe ↵")
 section below.

Edit

### Screenshots

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| [![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_media/plugin:addnewpage_nooptions.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_detail/plugin:addnewpage_nooptions.png?id=plugin%3Aaddnewpagedeluxe "plugin:addnewpage_nooptions.png") | The default form has a drop-down with all namespaces of the wiki and a text field for the pagename. |
| [![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_media/plugin:addnewpage_ns_hidden.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_detail/plugin:addnewpage_ns_hidden.png?id=plugin%3Aaddnewpagedeluxe "plugin:addnewpage_ns_hidden.png") | `{{NEWPAGE>test}}` with configuration option “Hide namespace selection” enabled. |
| [![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_media/plugin:addnewpage_ns_nothidden.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_detail/plugin:addnewpage_ns_nothidden.png?id=plugin%3Aaddnewpagedeluxe "plugin:addnewpage_ns_nothidden.png") | `{{NEWPAGE>test}}` with configuration option “Hide namespace selection” disabled. |
| [![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/exe/fetch.php?tok=2d306d&media=http%3A%2F%2Fi.imgur.com%2FgQ6yl0Y.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/exe/fetch.php?tok=2d306d&media=http%3A%2F%2Fi.imgur.com%2FgQ6yl0Y.png "http://i.imgur.com/gQ6yl0Y.png") | Parent namespaces are greyed-out where the user has no access to add pages. |

Edit

Edit

Syntax
------

Edit

### Basic

The basic syntax is:

{{NEWPAGE}} 

Or, to specify a namespace:

{{NEWPAGE>namespace}}

Where `namespace` is the target namespace for the new page. When this is specified, the namespace selection box can either be hidden (which it is by default) or set to show only sub-namespaces of the given one. This is configured in the main wiki configuration manager. If `@PAGE@` (or `@NS@`) is given, the current page ID (or its namespace) will be used (e.g. for use in [namespace templates](https://www.dokuwiki.org/namespace_templates "namespace_templates")
; see below).

Edit

### Creating a namespace

It is possible to use the `@INPUT@` placeholder to reference the user's input in the namespace specification. For example,

{{NEWPAGE>foo:@INPUT@:start}}

will create a new namespace within the _foo_ namespace.

Edit

### Date/time-based naming

The plugin supports usage of [strftime placeholders](https://www.php.net/manual/en/function.strftime.php "https://www.php.net/manual/en/function.strftime.php")
 in the namespace config. This allows adding date elements to the namespace specification

{{NEWPAGE>project:notes:@INPUT@\_%Y-%m-%d}}
{{NEWPAGE>reports:%Y:week\_%W}}

When used together with the [autopage](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpagedeluxe#configuration_and_settings "plugin:addnewpagedeluxe ↵")
 option to hide the input field, a daily page can be created with a single button click:

{{NEWPAGE>journal:%Y-%m-%d?autopage}}

Edit

### Configuration overrides

It is possible to override the plugin's [configuration](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpagedeluxe#configuration_and_settings "plugin:addnewpagedeluxe ↵")
 for a single usage of the syntax, by specifying a `?` followed by the config's name with an optional `no` prefix to negate it. Values for non-boolean configs can be provided with `=`, separated by `;`. Multiple overrides can be given, separated by `,` or `&`.

General syntax:

?\[no\]<option\_1>\[=value\_1\[;value\_2\]...\]\[,\[no\]<option\_2>\]...

Examples:

*   overwrite the default hide setting
    
    {{NEWPAGE>namespace?nohide}}
    
*   custom namespace exclusion and hide root
    
    {{NEWPAGE>namespace?exclude=project;reports&noshowroot}}
    

Edit

### New-page templates

This plugin is also compatible with the [newpagetemplate plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:newpagetemplate "plugin:newpagetemplate")
 (note that this is a separate feature to the [namespace templates](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpagedeluxe#namespace_templates "plugin:addnewpagedeluxe ↵")
 described below). To specify a template to be used with the new page, use one of the following syntaxes:

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| `{{NEWPAGE#newtpl}}` | Use the `newtpl` template |
| `{{NEWPAGE#newtpl1,newtpl2}}` | Present a drop-down list of `newtpl1` and  <br>`newtpl2`, allowing the user to choose one |
| `{{NEWPAGE#newtpl1\|Title One,newtpl2\|Title Two}}` | The same as above, but with nicer titles |

Edit

In all of these, the basic `>namespace` syntax can be included before the hash `#` character.

Edit

### Multiple occurences

The `{{NEWPAGE}}` syntax may be added multiple times on the same page.

Edit

### Site templates

To include an add-new-page form in a [Site Template](https://www.dokuwiki.org/template "template")
, use the following line:

<?php echo p\_render('xhtml',p\_get\_instructions('{{NEWPAGE}}'),$info) ?>	

Edit

### Namespace Templates

Add-new-page forms can also be added to [Namespace Templates](https://www.dokuwiki.org/namespace_templates "namespace_templates")
. The replacement patterns `@ID@` and `@NS@` can be used in the plugin's `namespace` parameter.

Edit

Configuration and Settings
--------------------------

You can configure the plugin in the Config Manager of DokuWiki. There are four settings:

1.  **Excluded namespaces** (`exclude`) — A semicolon-separated list of namespaces that should be excluded from the namespace selection list. Default: `wiki;playground`.
    
2.  **Show root namespace** (`showroot`) — Whether to show the wiki's root namespace in the namespace selection list. Default: true.
    
3.  **Hide namespace selection** (`hide`) — Hide the namespace selection list. Only applies if a namespace is given (see [above](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpagedeluxe#syntax "plugin:addnewpagedeluxe ↵")
    ). Default: true.
    
4.  **Hide “access denied” message** (`hideACL`) — Do not show the “You are not allowed to add pages” message when the user doesn't have permission to create pages. Default: false (i.e. do show the message).
    
5.  **Hide the input box** (`autopage`) — The preconfigured namespace is treated as a full page ID. Use with [date placeholders](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpagedeluxe#date_time-based_naming "plugin:addnewpagedeluxe ↵")
    ). Default: false.
    

Edit

FAQ
---

#### Why is a privileged user not allowed to add pages?

Logged in as administrator, why do I get a _You are not allowed to add pages_ error message ?

> This is a [known problem](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-addnewpage/issues/38 "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-addnewpage/issues/38")
> , caused by caching. As a workaround, add `~~NOCACHE~~` to the page that contains the form.

#### Addnewpage form on the admin page?

Is there a possibility to add the plugin as a part of the admin page, like the plugins pagemove or upgrade?

> Not currently, but this can be done by modifying your template and including the addnewpage form only when `$ACT=='admin`'

#### More than one form per page?

Is it possible to have more than one new-page form on a page?

> Yes. Nothing special needs to be done to permit this, just add the markup wherever you want the forms to appear.

#### Can the textbox be pre-populated with a value? Specifically, the ID variable? (Trying to have a user search for a page, then use this plugin to create the page if they don't find what they're looking for...)

#### How can a add\_page form add in the site template automaticaly? Where to place the php statements?

Edit

Development
-----------

Edit

plugin:breadcrumbdeluxe

### Table of Contents

*   [breadcrumbdeluxe Plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:breadcrumbdeluxe#breadcrumbdeluxe_plugin)
    
    *   [Installation](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:breadcrumbdeluxe#installation)
        
    *   [Examples/Usage](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:breadcrumbdeluxe#examples_usage)
        
    *   [Syntax](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:breadcrumbdeluxe#syntax)
        
    *   [Configuration and Settings](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:breadcrumbdeluxe#configuration_and_settings)
        
    *   [Development](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:breadcrumbdeluxe#development)
        
    *   [FAQ](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:breadcrumbdeluxe#faq)
        

breadcrumbdeluxe Plugin
=======================

Compatible with DokuWiki

*   2025-05-14 "Librarian" **unknown**
*   2024-02-06 "Kaos" **yes**
*   2023-04-04 "Jack Jackrum" **yes**
*   2022-07-31 "Igor" **yes**

*   [Download](https://github.com/C0DE8/dokuwiki-plugin-breadcrumbdeluxe/zipball/master)
    
*   [Report bugs](https://github.com/C0DE8/dokuwiki-plugin-breadcrumbdeluxe/issues)
    
*   [Donate](https://ko-fi.com/C0DE8)
    

[![plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/plugins/pluginrepo/images/dwplugin.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins)
 A better breadcrumb variant show page title rather than url key (from the original).

Last updated on

2024-02-29

Provides

[Syntax](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintype=1#extension__table "List all Syntax plugins")

Repository

[Source](https://github.com/C0DE8/dokuwiki-plugin-breadcrumbdeluxe/)

**By [C0DE8](mailto:dokuwiki%20%5Bdot%5D%20development%20%5Bat%5D%20c0de8%20%5Bdot%5D%20com "dokuwiki [dot] development [at] c0de8 [dot] com")
**

*   [addnewpagedeluxe](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpagedeluxe "plugin:addnewpagedeluxe")
    

Edit

Installation
------------

**External requirements:** { none }

Search and install the plugin using the [Extension Manager](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:extension "plugin:extension")
. Refer to [Plugins](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins "plugins")
 on how to install plugins manually.

# plugin:authwordpress [DokuWiki]

plugin:authwordpress

### Table of Contents

*   [WordPress Authentication Plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#wordpress_authentication_plugin)
    
    *   [Installation](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#installation)
        
        *   [Prerequisites](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#prerequisites)
            
        *   [Setup](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#setup)
            
        *   [Configuration and Settings](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#configuration_and_settings)
            
    *   [Usage](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#usage)
        
    *   [Development](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#development)
        
    *   [FAQ](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#faq)
        
        *   [I locked myself out, what do I do now?](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#i_locked_myself_out_what_do_i_do_now)
            

WordPress Authentication Plugin
===============================

Compatible with DokuWiki

*   2025-05-14 "Librarian" **probably**
*   2024-02-06 "Kaos" **yes**
*   2023-04-04 "Jack Jackrum" **yes**
*   2022-07-31 "Igor" **yes**

*   [Download](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/zipball/master)
    
*   [Report bugs](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/issues)
    
*   [Donate](https://github.com/sponsors/dregad)
    

[![plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/plugins/pluginrepo/images/dwplugin.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins)
 Provides authentication against a WordPress MySQL database backend

Last updated on

2025-01-05

Provides

[Auth](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintype=128#extension__table "List all Auth plugins")

Repository

[Source](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/)

Tagged with [authentication](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintag=authentication#extension__table "List all plugins with this tag")
, [wordpress](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintag=wordpress#extension__table "List all plugins with this tag")

**By [Damien Regad](mailto:dregad%20%5Bat%5D%20mantisbt%20%5Bdot%5D%20org "dregad [at] mantisbt [dot] org")
**

*   [newpagetemplate](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:newpagetemplate "plugin:newpagetemplate")
    
*   [cspheader](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:cspheader "plugin:cspheader")
    
*   [deletepagebutton](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:deletepagebutton "plugin:deletepagebutton")
    
*   [addnewpage](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpage "plugin:addnewpage")
    

Edit

Installation
------------

Edit

### Prerequisites

This plugin requires access to a WordPress site's MySQL database. See [Configuration and settings section below](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#configuration_and_settings "plugin:authwordpress ↵")
 for further information.

PHP's PDO extension is required.

#### Wordpress compatibility

The plugin supports WordPress releases 4.x 5.x and 6.x. It has been tested and confirmed to work on versions 4.3 to 4.9, 5.1 to 5.3 and 6.0 to 6.7.

**WARNING: not compatible with Wordpress 6.8**  
Wordpress [changed](https://make.wordpress.org/core/2025/02/17/wordpress-6-8-will-use-bcrypt-for-password-hashing/ "https://make.wordpress.org/core/2025/02/17/wordpress-6-8-will-use-bcrypt-for-password-hashing/")
 the password hashing algorithm in version 6.8, and the plugin does not support it yet; a community member contributed a [patch](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/pull/25 "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/pull/25")
 but it has not yet been reviewed and tested; feel free to use it (at your own risk) and provide any feedback you may have in the pull request.

Edit

### Setup

Search and install the plugin using the [Extension Manager](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:extension "plugin:extension")
. Refer to [Plugins](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins "plugins")
 on how to install plugins manually.

Edit

### Configuration and Settings

1.  Login to DokuWiki as administrator
    
2.  Go to your site's [Configuration Manager](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:config "plugin:config")
    
3.  In the _Authentication_ section
    
    *   Set _Authentication backend_ to **authwordpress**
        
    *   Add **@administrator** to the _Superuser_ list[1)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#fn__1)
        .  
        ![:!:](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/images/smileys/exclaim.svg) **failure to do so may leave you without any users having admin rights**, requiring you to manually edit the configuration file to fix the problem.
        
4.  Scroll down to the _Authwordpress_ Plugin section
    
    *   Provide the required configuration information
        
        *   hostname[2)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#fn__2)
            
        *   port number
            
        *   database name[3)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#fn__3)
            
        *   username[4)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#fn__4)
            
        *   password[5)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#fn__5)
            
        *   table prefix
            
5.  Save settings
    

Edit

Usage
-----

Navigate to your DokuWiki's _Login page_, and enter your WordPress credentials to login.

Logged in users' will have the following group memberships:

*   DokuWiki _default group_ (as defined in configuration),
    
*   a group matching their _Wordpress role_, i.e. one of
    
    *   administrator
        
    *   editor
        
    *   author
        
    *   contributor
        
    *   subscriber
        

These groups can be used to setup [ACL](https://www.dokuwiki.org/acl "acl")
 as appropriate.

The **plugin does not allow new users to sign up,** accounts must be created on the WordPress side.

Edit

Development
-----------

#### Revision history (Github releases)

*   [2025-01-05 (v1.3.0)](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/releases/tag/release_2025-01-05 "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/releases/tag/release_2025-01-05")
    
*   [2024-03-06 (v1.2.1)](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/releases/tag/release_2024-03-06 "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/releases/tag/release_2024-03-06")
    
*   [2023-06-06 (v1.2.0)](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/releases/tag/release_2023-06-06 "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/releases/tag/release_2023-06-06")
    
*   [2020-03-14 (v1.1.3)](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/releases/tag/release_2020-03-14 "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/releases/tag/release_2020-03-14")
    
*   [2016-03-03 (v1.1.2)](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/releases/tag/release_2016-03-03 "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/releases/tag/release_2016-03-03")
    

The complete [list of releases and change log](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/releases "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/releases")
 is available on Github.

     by dregad (2025-01-05 19:24)
    
*   [Update README.md](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/commit/dcac9e74df89c11d81f4f2d8c8f5708bcd7dcbab "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/commit/dcac9e74df89c11d81f4f2d8c8f5708bcd7dcbab")
     by dregad (2025-01-05 19:24)
    
*   [Fix checking of passwords containing \`\\\`, \`'\` or \`"\`](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/commit/666403eee7609fa4399ffdc03ed5366c8a77b99e "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/commit/666403eee7609fa4399ffdc03ed5366c8a77b99e")
     by dregad (2025-01-05 19:22)
    
*   [Apply WordPress latest changes to phpass class](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/commit/e74ba40746ec7d216e6ee800316592e4401623a8 "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/commit/e74ba40746ec7d216e6ee800316592e4401623a8")
     by dregad (2025-01-05 19:20)
    
*   [Bump version to 2024-03-06](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/commit/d7b2c9581ad47348560ad37d6277d29105c9bbec "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/commit/d7b2c9581ad47348560ad37d6277d29105c9bbec")
     by dregad (2024-03-06 19:47)
    

Full [Git log](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/commits/master "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/commits/master")
.

#### Known Bugs and Issues

Please refer to the plugin's [Issue tracker on Github](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/issues "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/issues")
.

#### ToDo/Wish List

*   Provide transparent authentication / single sign-on.  
    If user is already logged in WordPress, then they won't have to login again in DokuWiki, and vice-versa. Same thing for logout.
    

Edit

FAQ
---

Edit

### I locked myself out, what do I do now?

1.  Edit your local configuration file `./conf/local.php`
    
2.  Reset authentication to **authplain**
    
    $conf\['authtype'\] = 'authplain';
    
3.  Save the file
    

Edit

[1)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#fnt__1)

This will turn Wordpress administrators into DokuWiki super users

[2)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#fnt__2)
 , [3)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#fnt__3)
 , [4)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#fnt__4)
 , [5)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress#fnt__5)

mandatory

# plugin:deletepagebutton [DokuWiki]

plugin:deletepagebutton

### Table of Contents

*   [Delete Page Button Plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:deletepagebutton#delete_page_button_plugin)
    
    *   [Installation](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:deletepagebutton#installation)
        
    *   [Usage](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:deletepagebutton#usage)
        
    *   [Configuration and Settings](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:deletepagebutton#configuration_and_settings)
        
    *   [Development](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:deletepagebutton#development)
        
    *   [FAQ](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:deletepagebutton#faq)
        
    *   [Discussion](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:deletepagebutton#discussion)
        

Delete Page Button Plugin
=========================

Compatible with DokuWiki

*   2025-05-14 "Librarian" **probably**
*   2024-02-06 "Kaos" **yes**
*   2023-04-04 "Jack Jackrum" **yes**
*   2022-07-31 "Igor" **yes**

*   [Download](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-deletepagebutton/zipball/master)
    
*   [Report bugs](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-deletepagebutton/issues)
    
*   [Donate](https://github.com/sponsors/dregad)
    

[![plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/plugins/pluginrepo/images/dwplugin.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins)
 Adds a PageMenu button to Delete the current page

[![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/exe/fetch.php?w=220&tok=bba8c6&media=https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Fdregad%2Fdokuwiki-plugin-deletepagebutton%2Fmaster%2Fimages%2Fscreenshot.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/exe/fetch.php?tok=4b3f8b&media=https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Fdregad%2Fdokuwiki-plugin-deletepagebutton%2Fmaster%2Fimages%2Fscreenshot.png "Screenshot of deletepagebutton")

Last updated on

2022-06-08

Provides

[Action](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintype=4#extension__table "List all Action plugins")

Repository

[Source](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-deletepagebutton/)

Similar to [pagebuttons](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:pagebuttons "plugin:pagebuttons")

Tagged with [delete](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintag=delete#extension__table "List all plugins with this tag")
, [page](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintag=page#extension__table "List all plugins with this tag")

**By [Damien Regad](mailto:dregad%20%5Bat%5D%20mantisbt%20%5Bdot%5D%20org "dregad [at] mantisbt [dot] org")
**

*   [newpagetemplate](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:newpagetemplate "plugin:newpagetemplate")
    
*   [cspheader](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:cspheader "plugin:cspheader")
    
*   [authwordpress](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:authwordpress "plugin:authwordpress")
    
*   [addnewpage](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:addnewpage "plugin:addnewpage")
    

Edit

Installation
------------

Install the plugin using the [Extension Manager](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:extension "plugin:extension")
 and the download URL above, which points to latest version of the plugin. Refer to [Plugins](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins "plugins")
 on how to install plugins manually.

Edit

Usage
-----

On any page that the current user is allowed to edit, a _Delete_ button is displayed (trash can icon), allowing them to easily delete the page, without having to follow the cumbersome, officially [documented method](https://www.dokuwiki.org/page#delete_a_page "page")
.

[![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/exe/fetch.php?w=350&cache=recache&tok=756c24&media=https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Fdregad%2Fdokuwiki-plugin-deletepagebutton%2Fmaster%2Fimages%2Fscreenshot.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/exe/fetch.php?cache=recache&tok=4b3f8b&media=https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Fdregad%2Fdokuwiki-plugin-deletepagebutton%2Fmaster%2Fimages%2Fscreenshot.png "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-deletepagebutton/master/images/screenshot.png")

If JavaScript is enabled and the current [Template](https://www.dokuwiki.org/template "template")
 is supported by the plugin, a confirmation dialog will be displayed prior to deleting the page.

[![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/exe/fetch.php?w=300&cache=recache&tok=ede06a&media=https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Fdregad%2Fdokuwiki-plugin-deletepagebutton%2Fmaster%2Fimages%2Fconfirm.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/exe/fetch.php?cache=recache&tok=5a9303&media=https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Fdregad%2Fdokuwiki-plugin-deletepagebutton%2Fmaster%2Fimages%2Fconfirm.png "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-deletepagebutton/master/images/confirm.png")

Edit

Configuration and Settings
--------------------------

None

Edit

Development
-----------

*   [2021-03-01 (v1.2.0)](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-deletepagebutton/releases/tag/release_2021-03-01 "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-deletepagebutton/releases/tag/release_2021-03-01")
    
*   [2021-02-26 (v1.1.1)](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-deletepagebutton/releases/tag/release_2021-02-26 "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-deletepagebutton/releases/tag/release_2021-02-26")
    
*   [2021-02-19 (v1.1.0)](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-deletepagebutton/releases/tag/release_2021-02-19 "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-deletepagebutton/releases/tag/release_2021-02-19")
    
*   [2021-01-09 (v1.0.1)](https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-deletepagebutton/releases/tag/release_2021-01-09 "https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-deletepagebutton/releases/tag/release_2021-01-09")

 is available on Github.

# ✎ plugin:authwordpress [DokuWiki]

plugin:authwordpress

Edit the page and hit `Save`. See [syntax](https://www.dokuwiki.org/wiki:syntax "wiki:syntax")
 for Wiki syntax. Please edit the page only if you can **improve** it. If you want to test some things, learn to make your first steps on the [playground](https://www.dokuwiki.org/playground:playground "playground:playground")
.

[Media Files](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/exe/mediamanager.php?ns=plugin)

\====== WordPress Authentication Plugin ====== ---- plugin ---- description: Provides authentication against a WordPress MySQL database backend author : Damien Regad email : dregad@mantisbt.org type : auth lastupdate : 2025-01-05 compatible : Detritus, Elenor of Tsort, Frusterick Manners, Greebo, Hogfather, Igor, Jack Jackrum, Kaos+ depends : conflicts : similar : tags : wordpress, authentication downloadurl: https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/zipball/master bugtracker : https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/issues sourcerepo : https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/ donationurl: https://github.com/sponsors/dregad screenshot\_img : ---- ===== Installation ===== ==== Prerequisites ==== This plugin requires access to a WordPress site's MySQL database. See \[\[#configuration\_and\_settings|Configuration and settings section below\]\] for further information. PHP's PDO extension is required. === Wordpress compatibility === The plugin supports WordPress releases 4.x 5.x and 6.x. It has been tested and confirmed to work on versions 4.3 to 4.9, 5.1 to 5.3 and 6.0 to 6.7. \*\*WARNING: not compatible with Wordpress 6.8\*\* \\\\ Wordpress \[\[https://make.wordpress.org/core/2025/02/17/wordpress-6-8-will-use-bcrypt-for-password-hashing/|changed\]\] the password hashing algorithm in version 6.8, and the plugin does not support it yet; a community member contributed a \[\[https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/pull/25|patch\]\] but it has not yet been reviewed and tested; feel free to use it (at your own risk) and provide any feedback you may have in the pull request. ==== Setup ==== Search and install the plugin using the \[\[plugin:extension|Extension Manager\]\]. Refer to \[\[:Plugins\]\] on how to install plugins manually. ==== Configuration and Settings ==== - Login to DokuWiki as administrator - Go to your site's \[\[config|Configuration Manager\]\] - In the //Authentication// section \* Set //Authentication backend// to \*\*authwordpress\*\* \* Add \*\*@administrator\*\* to the //Superuser// list((This will turn Wordpress administrators into DokuWiki super users)).\\\\ :!: \*\*failure to do so may leave you without any users having admin rights\*\*, requiring you to manually edit the configuration file to fix the problem. - Scroll down to the //Authwordpress// Plugin section \* Provide the required configuration information \* hostname((mandatory)) \* port number \* database name((mandatory)) \* username((mandatory)) \* password((mandatory)) \* table prefix - Save settings ===== Usage ===== Navigate to your DokuWiki's //Login page//, and enter your WordPress credentials to login. Logged in users' will have the following group memberships: \* DokuWiki //default group// (as defined in configuration), \* a group matching their //Wordpress role//, i.e. one of \* administrator \* editor \* author \* contributor \* subscriber These groups can be used to setup \[\[:ACL\]\] as appropriate. The \*\*plugin does not allow new users to sign up,\*\* accounts must be created on the WordPress side. ===== Development ===== === Revision history (Github releases) === {{rss>https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/releases.atom 5}} The complete \[\[https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/releases|list of releases and change log\]\] is available on Github. === Recent commits === {{rss>https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/commits/master.atom 5 author date}} Full \[\[https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/commits/master|Git log\]\]. === Known Bugs and Issues === Please refer to the plugin's \[\[https://github.com/dregad/dokuwiki-plugin-authwordpress/issues|Issue tracker on Github\]\]. === ToDo/Wish List === \* Provide transparent authentication / single sign-on.\\\\ If user is already logged in WordPress, then they won't have to login again in DokuWiki, and vice-versa. Same thing for logout. ===== FAQ ===== ==== I locked myself out, what do I do now? ==== - Edit your local configuration file ''./conf/local.php'' - Reset authentication to \*\*authplain\*\* <code> $conf\['authtype'\] = 'authplain'; </code> - Save the file

Please fill all the letters into the box to prove you're human. I G P Y I  Please keep this field empty:

# Log In [DokuWiki]

plugin:authwordpress

Login
=====

You are currently not logged in! Enter your authentication credentials below to log in. You need to have cookies enabled to log in.

Log In Username   
Password   
 Remember me Log In

You can login with your [DokuWiki User Forum](http://forum.dokuwiki.org/)
 account here.

If you don't have an account just [register](https://forum.dokuwiki.org/)
 in the forum, then come back here.

# plugin:config [DokuWiki]

plugin:config

### Table of Contents

*   [Configuration Manager Plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:config#configuration_manager_plugin)
    
    *   [Description](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:config#description)
        
    *   [Usage](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:config#usage)
        
        *   [How is your config saved](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:config#how_is_your_config_saved)
            
        *   [Protecting Settings](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:config#protecting_settings)
            
        *   [File Permissions](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:config#file_permissions)
            
        *   [Triangle, Exclamation and Padlock Mark](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:config#triangle_exclamation_and_padlock_mark)
            

Configuration Manager Plugin
============================

Compatible with DokuWiki

(bundled)

[![plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/plugins/pluginrepo/images/dwplugin.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins)
 Manage your wiki's configuration settings (bundled with DokuWiki)

[![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_media/plugin:configmanager.png?w=220&tok=dd491d)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_media/plugin:configmanager.png "Screenshot of config")

Last updated on

2014-03-18

Provides

[Admin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintype=2#extension__table "List all Admin plugins")

Similar to [settingstree](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:settingstree "plugin:settingstree")

Tagged with [!bundled](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintag=%21bundled#extension__table "List all plugins with this tag")
, [configuration](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintag=configuration#extension__table "List all plugins with this tag")
, [options](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintag=options#extension__table "List all plugins with this tag")
, [settings](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintag=settings#extension__table "List all plugins with this tag")

Needed for [settingstree](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:settingstree "plugin:settingstree")

**By [Christopher Smith](mailto:chris%20%5Bat%5D%20jalakai%20%5Bdot%5D%20co%20%5Bdot%5D%20uk "chris [at] jalakai [dot] co [dot] uk")
**

*   [usermanager](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:usermanager "plugin:usermanager")
    
*   [source](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:source "plugin:source")
    
*   [googlemaps](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:googlemaps "plugin:googlemaps")
    
*   [iframe](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:iframe "plugin:iframe")
    
*   [linebreak](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:linebreak "plugin:linebreak")
    
*   [outdent](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:outdent "plugin:outdent")
    
*   [camelcase](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:camelcase "plugin:camelcase")
    
*   [xbr](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:xbr "plugin:xbr")
    
*   [box](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:box "plugin:box")
    
*   [sidebar](https://www.dokuwiki.org/template:sidebar "template:sidebar")
    
*   and 3 more

Edit

Description
-----------

[![The Configuration Manager - Field colors: blue = default, white = local change, light red = protected setting](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_media/plugin:configmanager.png?w=350&tok=ad5956 "The Configuration Manager - Field colors: blue = default, white = local change, light red = protected setting")](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_detail/plugin:configmanager.png?id=plugin%3Aconfig "plugin:configmanager.png")
 The Config Manager allows wiki [administrators](https://www.dokuwiki.org/config:superuser "config:superuser")
 to easily alter the wiki [configuration](https://www.dokuwiki.org/config "config")
 settings online from the comfort of their favorite web browser. This plugin manages basic DokuWiki configurations as well as [other settings](https://www.dokuwiki.org/devel:configuration "devel:configuration")
 for installed [plugins](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins "plugins")
 and currently used [template](https://www.dokuwiki.org/template "template")
.

Edit

Usage
-----

The Config Manager is accessible from the admin screen (Login as superuser, click “Admin” and choose “[![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/exe/fetch.php?w=14&tok=5dc818&media=https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Fdokuwiki%2Fdokuwiki%2Fmaster%2Flib%2Fplugins%2Fconfig%2Fadmin.svg)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/exe/fetch.php?tok=2ed489&media=https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Fdokuwiki%2Fdokuwiki%2Fmaster%2Flib%2Fplugins%2Fconfig%2Fadmin.svg "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dokuwiki/dokuwiki/master/lib/plugins/config/admin.svg")
 Configuration Settings”).

Settings are shown with different backgrounds to highlight their current status.

*   A blue background is used to show default values (`conf/dokuwiki.php`), eg. values that have not been modified by the user.
    
*   A white background indicates local changes (`conf/local.php`).
    
*   A light red background shows protected settings (`conf/local.protected.php`) which can not be modified.
    

Modify settings to your needs and save it by using the `Save` button at bottom of the page, by pressing 'Enter' in a textbox or by using the [accesskey](https://www.dokuwiki.org/accesskeys "accesskeys")
 of your browser: `<accesskey> + S`

Edit

### How is your config saved

On saving changed settings this plugin will copy the current local settings file (`conf/local.php`) to `conf/local.php.bak` and save the updated settings to `conf/local.php`. It will never make any changes to the default settings stored in `conf/dokuwiki.php`. However, any settings found in `conf/local.php` will override the default settings as explained in the [configuration options page](https://www.dokuwiki.org/config "config")
.

The plugin adds the following lines to the top of `conf/local.php` when it updates it:

[conf/local.php](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_export/code/plugin:config?codeblock=0 "Download Snippet")

/\*
 \* DokuWiki's Main Configuration File - Local Settings 
 \* Auto-generated by config plugin 
 \* Run for user: <username>
 \* Date: <current date/time, rfc 2822 format (day, dd MMM YYYY hh:mm:ss TZ)>
 \*/

Edit

### Protecting Settings

You can protect certain settings by placing them in `conf/local.protected.php` (create the file if it doesn't exist).

By default DokuWiki loads its configuration files in the following order:

1.  `conf/dokuwiki.php`
    
2.  `conf/local.php`
    
3.  `conf/local.protected.php`
    

This way, it is ensured that any values previously set from `conf/dokuwiki.php` and `conf/local.php` will be overridden by protected values from `conf/local.protected.php`. Any settings found in `conf/local.protected.php` will be displayed by the plugin surrounded in light-red to indicate their protected status. Editing of protected values is disabled.

The loading order of configuration files is controlled by the global `[$config_cascade](https://www.dokuwiki.org/devel:preload "devel:preload") ` variable.

#### Example

[conf/local.protected.php](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_export/code/plugin:config?codeblock=1 "Download Snippet")

<?php
/\*\*
 \* Protected settings
 \* Do override DokuWiki default settings and local settings from Config Manager
 \*/
$conf\['useacl'\] \= 1;

Edit

### File Permissions

To be able to save the configuration data, the file permissions of `conf/local.php` needs to be writable by the webserver, as well as the configuration directory itself. Refer to [permissions](https://www.dokuwiki.org/install:permissions "install:permissions")
 on details how set file permissions on various systems.

When the plugin detects that the config file can not be written, it will show a message box at the top of the page that says

> “The settings file can not be updated, if this is unintentional, ensure the local settings file name and permissions are correct.”

When this happens, the save button won't be shown.

Edit

### Triangle, Exclamation and Padlock Mark

Some fields in the configuration page show:

*   A yellow warning sign (![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_media/config:warning.png)) , or
    
*   An exclamation mark in a red circle (![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_media/config:danger.png)), or
    
*   The drawing of a padlock (![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_media/config:security.png))
    

These are the meanings of these three symbols:  
![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_media/config:warning.png) **Yellow Mark** ⇒ changing this option may result in undesirable behaviors.  
![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_media/config:danger.png) **Red circle** ⇒ changing this option could make your wiki and the configuration menu inaccessible.  
![](https://www.dokuwiki.org/_media/config:security.png) **Padlock** ⇒ changing this option could present a security risk.  

Please, keep attention when you change these fields.

Edit

# plugin:iframe [DokuWiki]

plugin:iframe

### Table of Contents

*   [iframe Plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:iframe#iframe_plugin)

        *   [Mixed Content (HTTPS vs. HTTP)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:iframe#mixed_content_https_vs_http)
            
    *   [Discussion](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:iframe#discussion)
        
    *   [Tip to use together with survey-tool](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:iframe#tip_to_use_together_with_survey-tool)

iframe Plugin
=============

Compatible with DokuWiki

*   2025-05-14 "Librarian" **unknown**
*   2024-02-06 "Kaos" **unknown**
*   2023-04-04 "Jack Jackrum" **unknown**
*   2022-07-31 "Igor" **yes**

*   [Download](https://github.com/Chris--S/dokuwiki-plugin-iframe/archive/master.zip)
    
*   [Report bugs](https://github.com/Chris--S/dokuwiki-plugin-iframe/issues)
    

[![plugin](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/plugins/pluginrepo/images/dwplugin.png)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins)
 Allows external URLs to be loaded into an iframe in your DokuWiki page

Last updated on

2023-08-17

Provides

[Syntax](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintype=1#extension__table "List all Syntax plugins")

Repository

[Source](https://github.com/Chris--S/dokuwiki-plugin-iframe)

Similar to [codebender](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:codebender "plugin:codebender")
, [iframeinterwiki](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:iframeinterwiki "plugin:iframeinterwiki")

Tagged with [embed](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintag=embed#extension__table "List all plugins with this tag")
, [iframe](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintag=iframe#extension__table "List all plugins with this tag")
, [include](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins?plugintag=include#extension__table "List all plugins with this tag")

**By [Christopher Smith](mailto:chris%20%5Bat%5D%20jalakai%20%5Bdot%5D%20co%20%5Bdot%5D%20uk "chris [at] jalakai [dot] co [dot] uk")
**

*   [usermanager](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:usermanager "plugin:usermanager")
    
*   [source](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:source "plugin:source")
    
*   [googlemaps](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:googlemaps "plugin:googlemaps")
    
*   [linebreak](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:linebreak "plugin:linebreak")
    
*   [outdent](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:outdent "plugin:outdent")
    
*   [camelcase](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:camelcase "plugin:camelcase")
    
*   [xbr](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:xbr "plugin:xbr")
    
*   [box](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:box "plugin:box")
    
*   [sidebar](https://www.dokuwiki.org/template:sidebar "template:sidebar")
    
*   [loginredirect](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:loginredirect "plugin:loginredirect")
    
*   and 3 more

This plugin allows you to embed other web sites in a DokuWiki page using an iframe.

Edit

Download and Installation
-------------------------

Search and install the plugin using the [Extension Manager](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:extension "plugin:extension")
. Refer to [Plugins](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugins "plugins")
 on how to install plugins manually.

Edit

### Revision History

For more recent revisions please check [github commit list](https://github.com/Chris--S/dokuwiki-plugin-iframe/commits/master "https://github.com/Chris--S/dokuwiki-plugin-iframe/commits/master")

*   2023-08-17
    
    *   fix PHP8 warnings
        
    *   add 'fullscreen' parameter
        
*   2016-02-02
    
    *   fix method signatures to match those of parent class
        
*   2015-08-09/10
    
    *   put plugin information into plug.info.txt
        
    *   add translation strings for French, German & Dutch
        
*   2015-07-31
    
    *   move code into github
        
*   2008-10-13
    
    *   more relaxed syntax
        
    *   new parameters
        
*   2006-12-17
    
    *   update for stricter type/error checking in php 5 (thanks Ilya)
        
    *   add support for controlling settings through config plugin
        
*   2006-05-01 — (darcs version only, others to follow) settings moved to use [common plugin functions](https://www.dokuwiki.org/devel:common_plugin_functions "devel:common_plugin_functions")
     making them editable via the [configuration manager](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:config "plugin:config")
     in the admin menu.
    
*   2005-10-17 — Released.
    

Edit

### Manual Installation

To install the plugin manually, download the source to your plugin folder, `lib/plugins` and extract its contents. That will create a new plugin folder, `lib/plugins/iframe`, and install the plugin.

The folder will contain:

conf/default.php                       default settings
conf/metadata.php                      settings information for the config plugin
lang/xx/lang.php                       language strings for config plugin
syntax.php                             plugin script

Edit

Syntax and Usage
----------------

Simple:

{{url>http://www.example.com/somepage.html}}

Complete Syntax:

{{url>someurl width,height noscroll noborder alignment fullscreen|alternate-text}}

That is an URL to embed, followed by several optional parameters. The following parameters are recognized:

*   _width_ and _height_ are optional. When only one value is given it is assumed to be the height. Otherwise the first value is width and the second value is height. You can give the values in `px`, `em`, `pt` or `%`. When no unit is given `px` ~is~ is NOT assumed (you have to give the value). Defaults values are: width - 98%, height - 400px.
    
*   `noscroll` will disable the scrollbars of the iframe
    
*   `noborder` will disable the frame border, note that your CSS might still add borders or may disable them without using this setting
    
*   Optionally an _alignment_ can be given as `left` or `right` to have the iframe floating to the left or right. Should be used with a width.
    
*   `fullscreen` will include the allowfullscreen attribute.
    
*   An _alternate text_ is optional. If not specified an empty string will be used.
    

See the plugin in action [here](http://wiki.jalakai.co.uk/dokuwiki/doku.php/test/iframe "http://wiki.jalakai.co.uk/dokuwiki/doku.php/test/iframe")
.

Edit

Configuration
-------------

The plugin has one configuration setting, which can be set via the admin/configuration settings page.

*   $js\_ok — default value - `false`, set to `true` to enable JavaScript URLs.
    

![:!:](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/images/smileys/exclaim.svg) This should never be enabled in public wikis as it opens a XSS security hole.

Edit

Acknowledgments
---------------

The plugin was created in response to an idea mentioned by [Styno](mailto:styno%20%5Bat%5D%20hotmail%20%5Bdot%5D%20com "styno [at] hotmail [dot] com")
.

This plugin was used as a basis for [google\_cal](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:google_cal "plugin:google_cal")
 by [Kite](mailto:Kite%20%5Bat%5D%20puzzlers%20%5Bdot%5D%20org "Kite [at] puzzlers [dot] org")

Edit

Known Problems
--------------

Edit

### Mixed Content (HTTPS vs. HTTP)

Modern web browsers will by default NOT embed stuff from “HTTP-only” sources in an [HTTPS](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HTTPS "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HTTPS")
 environment[1)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:iframe#fn__1)
 - browsers will show dialogs on top or bottom of the page's window (noted for IE and SeaMonkey) or a symbol at the beginning (Firefox) or the end (Chrome) of the URL bar, allowing situative or general settings for “mixed content”. ![:-D](https://www.dokuwiki.org/lib/images/smileys/biggrin.svg) Should be no problem if you keep your site consistent (use relative url paths for local sources), but may be confusing in test cases.

Edit

Discussion
----------

This used to be the right spot for this, but maybe not these days?

First a big thanks to Christopher for this plugins, it's because I want to use it a lot more in my newest wiki that I took the time to figure out why it was broken(it's not).

Some one (like me) upgrading their DokuWiki from the 2008-05-05 release using the older release of this plugin (2006-12-17) will think its broken.

I know I did, but after studying the source code and doing some experiments I realized what is really wrong is that the syntax changed, (a lot) between then and now. The old 2006 release **requires** brackets for its arguments, the 2008 release chokes on brackets and creates bad URL's. I suspect this is well known to people who follow the forums, but a surprise to those like me who simply visit the plugin pages every few years. To fix simply change your pages that use this plugin from using brackets to using a leading space and all will be well. Examples (all url's point to a private, off Internet server):

| Iframe release  <br>year | Example valid usage |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 2006-12-17 | {{url>http://asimov/DokuWiki/\[80%,400px\]\|iFrame not installed}} | OLD SYNTAX |
| 2008-10-31 | {{url>http://asimov/DokuWiki/ **80%,400px**\|iFrame not installed}} | NEW SYNTAX |
| 2008-10-31 | {{url>http://asimov/DokuWiki/\|iFrame not installed}} |     |
| 2006-12-17 | {{url>http://asimov/DokuWiki/\|iFrame not installed}} |     |

Edit

Notice how if no arguments are given, then the same syntax works fine for both releases. Perhaps this is why this plugin is tagged as 'broken' by some? Also keep in mind the original 2006 release only supported frame width, height. New version supports floats, and invisible borders. (which I think is really cool)

Regardless, it's a great plugin, thank you Christopher Smith for your work then and now.

PS. Yes I know doc above says the same thing, but sometimes you have to hit people over the head before they listen.

Edit

Tip to use together with survey-tool
------------------------------------

The iFrame is a great means to link your Wiki to for example Survey-tools like SurveyGizmo. In wiki's with registered users you can even use the username as a key to string together the two environments, by using the value of @USER@ as URL parameter sguid. The result is great, persistent dynamic content in the iFrame specific for the current user.

To achieve this, a very small addition to iFrame is needed so that it accepts @USER@ as a variable. In syntax.php, line 96 simply replace:

'src'   => $data\['url'\], 

with

'src'   => str\_replace('@USER@', $\_SERVER\['REMOTE\_USER'\], $data\['url'\]), 

That's all.

Independent from this: A useful extension of this plugin to avoid security problems would be, to restrict the possible URL's through a list in the configuration.

— [Harald](https://forum.dokuwiki.org/u/harald "https://forum.dokuwiki.org/u/harald")
 _2014/03/22 11:53_

Edit

Original size
-------------

I want to include an url with its current width and height. But I don't find any possibility to set parameters for this purpose.

— [Juergen](https://forum.dokuwiki.org/u/juergen "https://forum.dokuwiki.org/u/juergen")
 _2014/06/25 10:35_

> Unfortunately its not something that browsers support. Due to modern browser security models, its also not possible with javascript in most circumstances. — [ChrisS](https://forum.dokuwiki.org/u/ChrisS "https://forum.dokuwiki.org/u/ChrisS")
>  _2015-07-31 19:12_

Edit

[1)](https://www.dokuwiki.org/plugin:iframe#fnt__1)

older browsers may just inform the user about mixed content, but will mix it
